name: kettlebell thruster
instructions: Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in front of your chest with both hands, palms facing each other.
Lower into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping your chest up and your back straight.
As you reach the bottom of the squat, explosively drive through your heels to stand up, simultaneously pressing the kettlebell overhead.
Lock out your arms at the top of the movement, fully extending your elbows.
Lower the kettlebell back to the starting position by reversing the movement, bending your elbows and lowering the weight back to your chest.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Start by standing with your feet shoulder-width apart, holding a kettlebell in front of your chest with both hands, palms facing each other. Lower into a squat position by bending your knees and pushing your hips back, keeping your chest up and your back straight. As you reach the bottom of the squat, explosively drive through your heels to stand up, simultaneously pressing the kettlebell overhead. Lock out your arms at the top of the movement, fully extending your elbows. Lower the kettlebell back to the starting position by reversing the movement, bending your elbows and lowering the weight back to your chest. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
